pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh scripts
Confirmed in task.json, setup_task.sh, and export_result.sh scripts
EIA-310 standard confirmed by Wikipedia (19-inch rack article), RackSolutions, and multiple industry sources. 44.45mm = 1.75 inches (1U rack unit)
EIA-310 standard confirmed by Wikipedia (19-inch rack article), multiple industry sources, and EIA-310-D specification. 482.6mm = 19 inches (full rack width)
2mm aluminum sheet is a standard thickness for rack panel applications, confirmed as typical in README. Standard thickness for sheet metal fabrication.

Standard BNC panel mount connector cutout is 9.5 ± 0.2mm per IEC 61169-8 and confirmed by multiple manufacturers (Tyco Electronics, Amphenol, Vadcon). The 15.0mm claimed value contradicts the industry standard.

DB9 (DE-9) standard panel cutout width is 0.984 inches (24.99mm) per Winford Engineering technical specifications and manufacturer standards. The 31.6mm claimed value is significantly larger than the standard and contradicts IEC specifications.
DB9 (DE-9) standard panel cutout height is 0.492 inches (12.50mm) per Winford Engineering technical specifications. This matches the metadata claim exactly.
Task description specifies 2 BNC connector cutouts (15mm diameter each, positioned on left half) and 1 DE-9 rectangular cutout (right half). Total = 3 connector cutouts required.
EIA-310 standard requires 2 mounting holes per ear (left and right), totaling 4 holes. Setup_task.sh confirms M6 clearance holes at standard positions (31.75mm vertical centers per ear).

privileged_info_summary: The task describes a 1U 19-inch EIA-310 rack-mount panel design for FreeCAD with real standard dimensions: 1U height is 44.45mm, full rack width is 482.6mm, and 2mm aluminum thickness is standard for sheet metal. The design requires 4 EIA-310 mounting holes (2 per ear with 31.75mm vertical spacing) and 3 connector cutouts. However, two critical connector cutout dimensions in the task metadata are non-standard: BNC cutout claimed as 15.0mm diameter contradicts the IEC 61169-8 standard of 9.5±0.2mm, and DE-9 cutout width claimed as 31.6mm contradicts the standard 24.99mm (0.984 inches) width, though the height of 12.5mm is correct. These non-standard dimensions will cause fabrication and assembly issues if used for actual manufacturing.
